name: An Ancient Celtic Method of Divination / Celtic Cross
source_title: A. E. Waite, The Pictorial Key to the Tarot, Part III §7
license: Public domain in US (Sacred Texts/Wikisource public-domain notice)

positions.label: That covers him
positions.meaning: general influence affecting the person or matter of inquiry; the atmosphere in which other currents work

positions.label: What crosses him
positions.meaning: nature of the obstacles in the matter; if favourable, opposing forces may not be serious or may be good in itself but not productive of good in this connection

positions.label: What crowns him
positions.meaning: the Querent's aim or ideal in the matter; the best that can be achieved under the circumstances, but not yet actual

positions.label: What is beneath him
positions.meaning: foundation or basis of the matter; that which has already passed into actuality and which the Significator has made his own

positions.label: What is behind him
positions.meaning: the influence that is just passed, or is now passing away

positions.label: What is before him
positions.meaning: the influence that is coming into action and will operate in the near future

positions.label: Himself
positions.meaning: the Significator itself, whether person or thing, and its position or attitude in the circumstances

positions.label: His house
positions.meaning: environment and tendencies at work therein which affect the matter, such as position in life and immediate friends

positions.label: His hopes or fears
positions.meaning: hopes or fears in the matter

positions.label: What will come
positions.meaning: final result or culmination brought about by the influences shown by the other cards
